<div className="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2"><Badge variant="outline">NA-AI Landing</Badge><Badge>Customization</Badge></div>

## Customize without rewriting every component

NA-AI Landing is data-driven by design. Most product copy and section content lives in:

```txt
src/@data
```

This makes the template easier to adapt because you can update the product story first, then adjust layout and visuals only where needed.

<Callout title="Core idea">
  Start by changing the data, not the components. Rewrite the product story before you rewrite the UI.
</Callout>

## Recommended customization order

<Steps>
  <Step>
  ### Update site settings
Edit `src/config/site.ts` for product name, tagline, site URL, SEO title, and social preview settings.
</Step>
  <Step>
  ### Update core content
Edit files under `src/@data`, starting with hero, features, pricing, testimonials, FAQ, CTA, and contact.
</Step>

  <Step>
  ### Replace assets
Update logo, icons, Open Graph images, Twitter images, and manifest assets.
</Step>

  <Step>
  ### Adjust branding
Update fonts, token usage, theme settings, and product-specific visuals only after the content direction is clear.
</Step>

  <Step>
  ### Test and deploy
Run the build, inspect both themes, and test responsive layouts before deployment.
</Step>

</Steps>

## Data files

| Section | File |
| --- | --- |
| Hero | `src/@data/hero.ts` |
| Features | `src/@data/features.ts` |
| Integrations | `src/@data/integrations.ts` |
| Pricing | `src/@data/pricing.ts` |
| Pricing Table | `src/@data/pricing-table.ts` |
| Testimonials | `src/@data/customers.ts` |
| FAQ | `src/@data/faq.ts` |
| CTA | `src/@data/cta.ts` |
| Contact | `src/@data/contact.ts` |
| Footer | `src/@data/footer.ts` |
| Navbar | `src/@data/navbar.ts` |

## Site configuration

Edit:

```txt
src/config/site.ts
```

Use it to update product name, tagline, site URL, SEO title, SEO description, Open Graph image, and Twitter image.

<Callout type="info" title="SEO rule">
  Update the site URL and social preview assets before deploying publicly.
</Callout>

## Branding and tokens

NA-AI Landing uses:

```txt
@pycolors/ui
@pycolors/tokens
```

The shared tokens provide a stable visual foundation for colors, surfaces, borders, radius, shadows, focus states, and dark/light mode.

<Callout title="Design rule">
  Prefer semantic tokens over raw color values. It keeps the page consistent across light and dark mode.
</Callout>

## Content strategy

<PreferAvoid
  prefer={[
    'clear product promise',
    'specific AI or SaaS use case',
    'short feature labels',
    'proof-oriented sections',
    'pricing aligned with your offer',
  ]}
  avoid={[
    'generic AI buzzwords',
    'changing every component first',
    'too many competing CTAs',
    'removing trust sections',
    'publishing with default SEO assets',
  ]}
/>

## Customize pricing

Pricing content is split across:

```txt
src/@data/pricing.ts
src/@data/pricing-table.ts
```

Use it to update plan names, monthly/yearly pricing, comparison rows, feature availability, CTA labels, and positioning copy.

<Callout title="Pricing rule">
  Pricing should clarify the buying decision, not just list features.
</Callout>

## Customize SEO and assets

Update social preview images:

```txt
public/seo/og-main.jpg
public/seo/twitter-main.jpg
```

Update icons:

```txt
public/icons
```

Update manifest:

```txt
public/manifest.webmanifest
```

## Common questions

<Accordions type="single">
  <Accordion title="Can I use this for a non-AI SaaS?">Yes. NA-AI is optimized for AI and analytics positioning, but the structure works well for many B2B SaaS products.</Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Can I add a backend later?">Yes. Keep API logic outside the presentational section components and connect forms or analytics through dedicated modules.</Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Should I replace PyColors UI?">Usually no. Keep PyColors UI and tokens unless you have a strong reason to rebuild the UI foundation.</Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Can I remove sections?">Yes. Remove sections only after checking that the page still explains the offer, proof, pricing, objections, and next action clearly.</Accordion>
</Accordions>
